rule: Keep navigation flat and clear — no more than 5 top-level items for GRM website
do_pattern: Home / The Closing Table / The Front Porch / Advertise / Contact — five items maximum. Logo links to home. Teal CTA button for Advertise in nav.
dont_pattern: Never use dropdown mega-menus. Never hide key pages in nested navigation. Never put Contact behind a hamburger menu on desktop.
notes: GRM website has a simple structure. Agents and homeowners should find their publication in one click from anywhere.

rule: Navigation must clearly separate the two audiences from the first glance
do_pattern: Two distinct publication names visible in nav. Consider visual differentiation — Closing Table in one style, Front Porch in another.
dont_pattern: Never group both publications under a generic 'Publications' dropdown that requires a second click to differentiate.
notes: Self-selection is the most important UX job on this site.

rule: Never set Comfortaa below 16px on screen — it loses legibility at small sizes
do_pattern: Use Comfortaa at 24px and above for headers. Drop to Nunito for any text below 18px.
dont_pattern: Never use Comfortaa for body text, captions, or small labels. It is a display font.
notes: Comfortaa is rounded and distinctive at display size. It muddies at small sizes.

rule: Body text minimum 16px on screen for Nunito — 14.5px is the print equivalent
do_pattern: Set base body font-size: 16px in CSS. Line-height 1.6 for Nunito body text.
dont_pattern: Never set body text below 16px on screen. Never set line-height below 1.4 for any paragraph text.
notes: Accessibility and readability. GRM's agent audience includes people in their 50s-60s.

rule: Maintain strict typographic hierarchy across all pages
do_pattern: H1: Comfortaa Bold 48px+ / H2: Comfortaa Bold 32px / H3: Comfortaa Semibold 24px / Body: Nunito Regular 16px / Caption: Nunito Regular 12px
dont_pattern: Never skip heading levels. Never use font-size alone to create hierarchy — use the correct HTML heading level too.
notes: Heading hierarchy affects both visual clarity and SEO.

rule: Always verify color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA minimum
do_pattern: Near-black #1A1A1A on Warm Cream #FAF8F4 = 16.7:1 ratio (passes). White #FFFFFF on Teal #52B5CB = 3.2:1 (use for large text only, 18px+)
dont_pattern: Never use teal #52B5CB as body text color on cream — it fails WCAG AA for small text. Never use dusty rose as text on white.
notes: Teal is an accent and CTA color — not a body text color. This is a common mistake.

rule: Use teal sparingly as a true accent — maximum 2-3 teal elements per section
do_pattern: One teal CTA button per section. One teal accent line or divider. One teal label or badge if needed.
dont_pattern: Never flood a section with teal. Never use teal as a background for full content sections. Never use three competing teal elements at the same visual weight.
notes: Teal loses its power as an accent when overused.

rule: Mobile layout must stack vertically — publication cards, team photos, and stats go single column
do_pattern: Use CSS Grid or Flexbox with responsive breakpoints. Test at 375px (iPhone SE), 768px (iPad), 1280px (desktop).
dont_pattern: Never rely on horizontal side-by-side layouts that collapse poorly on mobile. Never use fixed pixel widths on content sections.
notes: Marion County's real estate agent audience uses mobile heavily. A broken mobile layout kills credibility.

rule: Section spacing must be generous — 80px vertical padding between major sections on desktop
do_pattern: Use 80px top and bottom padding on full-width sections. 48px between card grid items. 32px inside card padding.
dont_pattern: Never compress sections to save space. Whitespace signals premium quality. A crowded layout feels cheap.
notes: Em Agency's design language is generous with whitespace. The CSV files confirm this.

rule: Hero section must be full viewport height on desktop with the Marion County photo
do_pattern: min-height: 100vh on the hero. Photo fills the space with object-fit: cover. Logo and headline positioned in the lower third or center.
dont_pattern: Never use a short hero that makes the page feel like a landing page template. Never use a generic gradient background as a hero substitute.
notes: The 1-Horse-Country-1.jpeg photo is too strong to waste in a small space.

rule: Contact and inquiry forms must be simple — name, email, company, message, inquiry type
do_pattern: Four to five fields maximum. Inquiry type as a select: Advertise / General / Media Inquiry. Teal submit button. Clear placeholder text in Nunito.
dont_pattern: Never add unnecessary fields (phone number is optional). Never use CAPTCHA without strong spam evidence. Never auto-scroll to form without user intent.
notes: Every extra field reduces form completion rate. Keep it minimal.

rule: Each section should have one primary CTA — not two or three competing actions
do_pattern: One teal button per section. If a secondary link is needed, style it as an underlined text link in teal — not a second button.
dont_pattern: Never place two equal-weight teal buttons in the same section. Never use three CTAs on the same screen view.
notes: Decision paralysis. One clear next step per section.

rule: Use subtle entrance animations — content that fades in as it scrolls into view
do_pattern: Fade-in with slight upward translate: opacity 0 to 1, translateY 20px to 0, 0.4s ease-out. Trigger on IntersectionObserver.
dont_pattern: Never use heavy animations that delay content visibility. Never animate more than 3 elements simultaneously. Never animate on every scroll.
notes: Animations should feel editorial and refined, not startup-flashy.

rule: Images must be optimized before deployment
do_pattern: Convert JPEGs to WebP format. Compress to under 200KB for hero images. Use lazy loading for below-fold images. Use srcset for responsive images.
dont_pattern: Never deploy the raw camera JPEGs from Em Agency — they are 5-10MB each. Never use a single massive image without responsive variants.
notes: The DSC files from the photo shoot are full-resolution camera files. They need compression before web use.

rule: Every page needs a unique title tag and meta description referencing Marion County
do_pattern: Title: The Closing Table | Marion County Real Estate Magazine | Get Rooted Media. Meta: 150-160 characters, include Marion County and the specific publication value proposition.
dont_pattern: Never use generic titles like 'Home Page' or 'Publications'. Never duplicate meta descriptions across pages.
notes: Marion County is a strong local SEO keyword. Use it consistently.

rule: All images need descriptive alt text referencing Marion County context
do_pattern: Alt text: 'Two horses grazing at golden hour on a Marion County horse farm with Spanish moss trees in background' — specific and descriptive.
dont_pattern: Never use generic alt text like 'image' or 'photo'. Never leave alt text empty on meaningful images.
notes: Descriptive alt text also helps with local SEO.
